title: Customer escalation and incident coordination
business_context: A fictional priority account reports intermittent transaction failures while engineering teams see conflicting service indicators.
initial_stakes: A priority service commitment is at risk and customer operations needs accurate, controlled communication.

turns.situation: Support reports intermittent failures while dashboards show only partial impact.

options.label: Correlate customer timestamps with service traces
options.description: Validate the reported path against operational evidence.
options.expected_outcome: The team confirms the affected transaction path.

options.label: State that the platform is healthy
options.description: Dismiss the escalation because aggregate dashboards look normal.
options.expected_outcome: Customer trust and incident detection both deteriorate.

turns.situation: Trace correlation confirms a narrow but material order-processing failure.

options.label: Open incident command and name service owners
options.description: Coordinate engineering, support, and product around one timeline.
options.expected_outcome: Operational ownership and customer checkpoints become consistent.

options.label: Route the issue between teams
options.description: Continue bilateral handoffs without a shared incident owner.
options.expected_outcome: Resolution time and message inconsistency increase.

turns.situation: The customer requests a recovery estimate before the fault is fully isolated.

options.label: Communicate impact, uncertainty, and next checkpoint
options.description: Give a factual update without promising an unsupported recovery time.
options.expected_outcome: The customer receives a credible operational update.

options.label: Promise recovery within fifteen minutes
options.description: Provide a fixed estimate without sufficient evidence.
options.expected_outcome: An unsupported commitment increases commercial pressure.

turns.situation: A service configuration issue is isolated and a controlled correction is available.

options.label: Apply correction with telemetry checkpoints
options.description: Change the affected path and verify each recovery signal.
options.expected_outcome: The fault is corrected with evidence at each checkpoint.

options.label: Apply the correction across every service
options.description: Expand the change beyond the confirmed fault domain.
options.expected_outcome: The recovery change creates unnecessary platform risk.

turns.situation: Transactions are succeeding and the incident can move into monitored recovery.

options.label: Restore service and maintain a customer checkpoint
options.description: Confirm recovery evidence and keep one final monitoring window.
options.expected_outcome: Service and customer confidence recover together.

options.label: Close the escalation immediately
options.description: End communication before the monitoring window completes.
options.expected_outcome: Technical recovery continues without a final confidence checkpoint.
